provenance.title: Caduceus: Bi-Directional Equivariant Long-Range DNA Sequence Modeling
provenance.abstract: Caduceus is the first family of RC (reverse-complement) equivariant bi-directional long-range DNA language models. It builds on the Mamba block, extending it to BiMamba (parameter-efficient bi-directionality via weight-tied forward/reverse Mamba) and MambaDNA (adds RC equivariance via channel split + shared-parameter processing of a sequence and its reverse complement). Two variants: Caduceus-PS (RC equivariance via parameter sharing, incl. RC-equivariant embedding and LM head) and Caduceus-Ph (BiMamba stack + RC data augmentation + post-hoc conjoining at inference). Pre-trained with MLM on the human reference genome, Caduceus outperforms similarly-sized HyenaDNA/Mamba and, on long-range variant effect prediction, exceeds 10x larger models that lack bi-directionality or equivariance.
key_claims: BiMamba: parameter-efficient bi-directional Mamba via weight-tied in/out projections (forward + flipped-reverse added)
MambaDNA: RC-equivariance inductive bias via channel split + RC on one half + shared Mamba params, without doubling channel dim
Caduceus-PS is the first RC-equivariant LM pre-training paradigm (RC-equivariant embedding, MambaDNA stack, RC-equivariant LM head)
Caduceus-Ph (post-hoc conjoining: average model on seq and its RC at inference) is best-overall on Genomic Benchmarks
On long-range VEP (>100k bp to TSS), Caduceus-PS beats NT-v2 (500M) and even Enformer despite being 1.9M params

architecture.primary_variant: Caduceus-Ph (BiMamba stack, 4 layers, d_model 256, RC data-aug + post-hoc conjoining) for NT tasks; Caduceus-PS for VEP

modules.name: RCEquivariantEmbedding
modules.notes: Only used in Caduceus-PS. Caduceus-Ph uses a standard nn.Embedding + RC data augmentation.

modules.name: Mamba
modules.notes: Standard Gu&Dao selective SSM block: in-proj to 2D, causal conv, SiLU, selective SSM, gated MLP, out-proj.

modules.name: BiMamba

parameters.weight_tie: in/out projections shared between forward and reverse

modules.notes: Apply shared Mamba to x and to flip(x); flip the reverse output back and add. Half the params of a naive 2x bi-directional model.

modules.name: MambaDNA
modules.notes: Wraps Mamba or BiMamba. Split channels; apply shared sequence operator to first half and to RC of second half; RC the second output back; concat. Satisfies RC equivariance (Thm 3.1).

modules.name: RCEquivariantLMHead
modules.notes: Caduceus-PS only. Vocab is the 4 nucleotides (+ special tokens) at char-level.

modules.name: MLMHead
modules.notes: Masked-LM pre-training head (BERT recipe). Vocab is char-level (A/C/G/T/N + special).

differences.equivariance: parameter sharing (RC-equivariant embed + MambaDNA + RC-equivariant LM head)
differences.downstream: split final hidden, average the two halves for RC-invariance
RC-ensemble at inference (average f(x) and f(RC(x)))

mathematical_spec.name: Selective SSM recurrence (discretized)
mathematical_spec.latex: h_{t+1} = \bar{A} h_t + \bar{B} x_t, \quad y_{t+1} = C h_t + D x_t

training_pipeline.effective_batch_size: 2^20 tokens per batch (held constant across seq lengths)
data_augmentation: RC data augmentation (all models except Caduceus-PS): with prob 0.5 replace sequence by its reverse complement
training_pipeline.curriculum: Pre-train on HG38 human reference genome (Enformer splits: 34,021 segments extended to 2^20 bp, ~35B tokens). BERT MLM masking recipe (15%). Beta values note: paper states beta1=0.95, beta2=0.9 in App C.

datasets.name: Variant Effect Prediction (Avsec/Enformer, via Trop et al. 2023)

evaluation_protocol.compute: Pre-training on 3090/A5000/A6000/V100/A100 GPUs (device count varies by model size). Downstream fine-tuning fits on a single mid-range GPU (models are 0.5M-7.7M params).
special_conditions: 5-fold CV (Genomic Benchmarks) / 10-fold CV (NT tasks) with different seeds
mean pooling of final hidden states for classification
Caduceus-PS: split final hidden and average halves for RC-invariance
Caduceus-Ph: RC-ensemble (average f(x), f(RC(x))) at inference

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Load official Caduceus weights from HuggingFace (kuleshov-group org) via AutoModel(trust_remote_code=True) rather than pretraining
implementation_assumptions.basis: pre-training on 35B tokens across many GPUs is infeasible to reproduce; weights + custom modeling code are public on the Hub

implementation_assumptions.assumption: The mamba-ssm / causal-conv1d CUDA kernels are required for the official Caduceus forward pass (Mamba selective scan)
implementation_assumptions.basis: Caduceus is built on the Mamba block which ships fused CUDA kernels; CPU fallback is slow/limited

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Downstream classification = mean-pool final hidden states + linear head; Caduceus-PS averages the two channel-split halves first
Char-level tokenizer (A/C/G/T/N + special) bundled with the released weights

ambiguities.description: Main text implies AdamW; App C states beta1=0.95, beta2=0.9 (unusual ordering). Downstream fine-tuning betas not separately specified.
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Use HF Trainer defaults for fine-tuning (beta1=0.9, beta2=0.999); pre-training uses App C values.
alternatives: match App C for fine-tuning too

ambiguities.description: Genomic Benchmarks LR grid-searched in {1e-3, 2e-3} per task/model (Table 5); a single reproduction run must pick one.
Paper reports both; Ph is best-overall on Genomic Benchmarks, PS best on long-range VEP.
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Default to Caduceus-Ph for classification tasks (best overall)
